metadata.title: Zimmerman Unified Geometry Framework (ZUGF): Validated Peptide Therapeutics Pipeline with MS QA/QC
metadata.description:
LEGAL DISCLAIMERS

IMPORTANT - READ BEFORE USE:

  • NO THERAPEUTIC CLAIMS: This software and all associated molecular sequences are provided for theoretical and computational research purposes only. No therapeutic, diagnostic, or medicinal claims are made or implied.
  • NOT FOR HUMAN USE: The peptide sequences described herein have NOT been tested in humans or animals. They are computational predictions only and MUST NOT be administered to any living organism without extensive regulatory approval.
  • EXPERIMENTAL VALIDATION REQUIRED: All molecular sequences require rigorous experimental validation, toxicology studies, and regulatory approval before any clinical consideration.
  • UNPROVEN HYPOTHESIS: The Z² = 32π/3 geometric hypothesis underlying this framework remains unproven and has not been independently validated by peer review.
  • NO WARRANTY: This software is provided "AS IS" without warranty of any kind, express or implied, including but not limited to warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, and noninfringement.
  • LIMITATION OF LIABILITY: In no event shall the author be liable for any claim, damages, or other liability arising from the use of this software or any molecular sequences derived from it.
  • RESEARCH USE ONLY: This work is intended solely for academic and research purposes. Any commercial use requires separate licensing arrangements.

ABSTRACT

This repository contains the computational pipeline for testing the hypothesis that biological geometry scales from vacuum constants via a Thermodynamic Expansion Multiplier (1.0391). We derive that biology operates approximately 3.91% expanded from vacuum Z² constants due to 310K temperature and water solvation effects.

VERSION 3.0 - PEPTIDE THERAPEUTICS

This version includes CRO-ready synthesis specifications for three computationally-designed peptide candidates:

  • ZIM-ALZ-001 (WFFY): Tau protein binder - Alzheimer's Disease research - [M+H]+ = 703.32 Da
  • ZIM-SYN-004 (FPF): Alpha-synuclein binder - Parkinson's Disease research - [M+H]+ = 451.23 Da
  • ZIM-ADD-003 (RWWFWR): nAChR modulator - Addiction research - [M+H]+ = 1035.54 Da

All peptide sequences are validated using RDKit for chemical integrity (proper amide bonds, L-stereochemistry) and include theoretical mass spectrometry isotope distributions for CRO QA/QC verification.

KEY COMPUTATIONAL FINDINGS

  1. Thermodynamic Expansion Multiplier: 1.0391 ± 0.0367 (calculated from empirical protein and DNA data)
  2. Kinetic Energy Test: Hyperthermophile proteins show 2.57% more expanded geometry than psychrophile proteins (p ≈ 0)
  3. Allosteric Z² Convergence: 2/3 proteins show HOLO state closer to Z² than APO state (empirical PDB analysis)
  4. Decoy Falsification: Pipeline correctly rejects impossible peptides (poly-tryptophan: Z² fitness 0%)
  5. Mass Spectrometry Validation: All three peptide candidates pass RDKit chemical validation

LICENSING

  • Software: AGPL v3.0 (copyleft) - modifications must be open-sourced
  • Molecular Sequences: CC0 Public Domain - free for any use without restriction
metadata.notes: LEGAL DISCLAIMER: This is theoretical and computational research ONLY. NO THERAPEUTIC CLAIMS are made. All molecular sequences are UNVALIDATED computational predictions and MUST NOT be used in humans or animals without extensive experimental validation and regulatory approval. The Z² hypothesis remains UNPROVEN. This software is provided AS IS without warranty. The author assumes NO LIABILITY for any use of this software or derived molecular sequences. Peptide sequences are released to CC0 Public Domain.
